title: mammillaria saboae subsp. haudeana
intro: Mammillaria saboae subsp. haudeana is a rare and endangered species of cactus, commonly known as the Haude's pincushion cactus, belonging to the Mammillaria genus. This plant is traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Mammillaria saboae subsp. haudeana include its potential to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations, as well as its use in traditional remedies for digestive issues and fever reduction. Herbal preparations of this cactus can be made by drying and grinding the stems, which can then be used to make teas, ointments, or salves for topical application.
